name: Paddle API
what_it_does: Paddle's billing and payments API acting as the merchant of record, handling global tax compliance, subscriptions, and checkout for software and SaaS businesses.
use_cases: Automating subscription lifecycle management via agents
Processing global payments with automatic tax handling
Managing customer billing and invoice generation
Handling plan upgrades, downgrades, and cancellations
Building SaaS billing dashboards with agent-retrieved data
not_for: Businesses needing full payment method customization
Teams wanting direct payment processor relationships
Low-margin businesses where Paddle's fees reduce profitability
best_when: A SaaS or digital product business needs global payment processing with automatic tax compliance handled by Paddle as the merchant of record.
avoid_when: You sell physical goods, need direct processor control, or have very tight margins.

auth.notes: API key authentication with separate sandbox and production keys. Simple bearer token auth. Keys generated from Paddle dashboard. No fine-grained scopes — keys grant full access.

pricing.paid_tiers: 5% + $0.50 per transaction (Starter), custom rates for higher volume
pricing.notes: Revenue share model — Paddle takes a percentage. Higher per-transaction rate than Stripe but eliminates tax compliance overhead. Sandbox is free for testing.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error responses include type and detail fields. Validation errors include specific field errors. Documentation has improved significantly with Paddle Billing (v2) API.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Some endpoints support idempotency. Subscription management operations are generally safe to retry.
known_agent_gotchas: Paddle Billing (v2) and Paddle Classic are two different APIs — ensure using correct version
As merchant of record, Paddle controls the checkout experience — customization is limited
Transaction approval process for new accounts can take time
Webhook payloads must be validated using Paddle's public key signature
Tax handling is automatic but may conflict with manual tax configurations
Sandbox environment has limitations compared to production (e.g., limited payment methods)
